title: Barcodes in Topology and Analysis
speaker: Egor Shelukhin
abstract: Persistence modules and their associated barcodes were intensively studied since the early 2000s with a view towards applied mathematics. Recently they have also found numerous applications in pure mathematics. We will discuss a few examples from symplectic topology and function theory where persistence modules play an important role.

proof_stages.label: Barcode formalism and stability
proof_stages.summary: Sublevel-set persistence is abstracted to persistence modules, decomposed into interval summands called barcodes, and related to C0 perturbations by bottleneck stability, which solves the motivating circle-height obstruction.

proof_stages.label: Delta-deep nodal topology estimates
proof_stages.summary: The talk formulates the spectral-topological problem for Laplace eigenfunctions, explains why ordinary nodal counts are hopeless in higher dimensions, introduces delta-deep nodal domains, and states Sobolev and spectral upper bounds for their counts.

proof_stages.label: Barcode-counting proof of deep nodal bounds
proof_stages.summary: Deep Betti and nodal counts are reduced to the number of long bars, then bounded using barcode stability, Mayer-Vietoris/exact-sequence subadditivity, polynomial critical-point estimates on cubes, Sobolev approximation, and dyadic partitions.

proof_stages.label: Floer barcodes for Hamiltonian fixed points
proof_stages.summary: After setting up Hamiltonian dynamics and the Conley/Hofer-Zehnder context, the speaker states the CP^n excess-fixed-point theorem and packages Floer homology as a barcode whose endpoints are fixed points and whose finite bars have controlled length.

proof_stages.label: Iteration-growth proof of new periodic points
proof_stages.summary: Linear growth of total Floer-bar length under prime iteration, together with the uniform CP^n bar-length bound, forces the number of finite bars to grow linearly and hence produces new simple periodic points.

proof_stages.label: Hamiltonian-homeomorphism Hilbert-Smith setup
proof_stages.summary: The Hilbert-Smith conjecture and its p-adic reduction are reviewed, Hamiltonian homeomorphisms are introduced as a C0-closure regularity class, and extended Floer barcodes plus the spectral norm gamma supply the finiteness and norm properties needed for the symplectic theorem.

proof_stages.label: P-adic barcode iteration contradiction
proof_stages.summary: A prime-iteration barcode inequality for the spectral norm is combined with p-adic roots and primes in arithmetic progressions to force infinitely many long bars, contradicting the finiteness property and ruling out the p-adic action.
